title: salvia apiana var. typica
intro: Salvia apiana var. typica, also known as White Sage, is a perennial herb native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico, belonging to the mint family Lamiaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The health benefits of White Sage include its ability to improve cognitive function, boost mood, and support digestive health, making it a popular herb in herbal remedies. White Sage is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or smudge stick, and can also be used in traditional ceremonies and rituals for its purifying and protective properties.
